calendar.matters:
Co Trustee John Melones Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-31-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for John Melone.

The Court stated that it reviewed the settlement agreement and that everything was in order. Mr. Freedman stated that the settlement agreement had been signed by all of the Parties and by all of the interested Beneficiaries. The Court approved the settlement agreement, (subject to review of the written order). The Petition For Instructions was taken off calendar.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition For Instructions Re Hand Written Instrument
Off Calendar Aug-31-2009 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions Re Hand Written Instrument
Off Calendar Jun-16-2009 Continued To Aug-31-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Compensation Of Attorney Of Co Trustee Norman Arias
Off Calendar Apr-09-2009 Advanced From Jun-15-09 To May-05-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Re Hand Written Instrument
Off Calendar May-11-2009 Continued To Jun-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Judge David L. Ballati, Clerk: Vicki Mack, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 (Department 318)

The cause comes on calendar, as continued from May 7, 2009, for the purpose of receiving the executed written agreement or going forth with the Probate Court Trial on the issue re the validity of the June 20, 2005 7th Amendment to the Trust in the Matter of the 10/17/03 Revocable Trust of NATALIA S. CAPILI.

Appearances:
Charles Bruce Hamilton, Esq. is present, counsel for co-trustee Norman Arias (not present).
Michael Ellis Freedman, Esq. is present, counsel for co-trustee John Melone, (not present).

Respective counsel represent that a written agreement has been signed by the co-trustees, Norman Arias and John Melone as well as Leila Osler, Ms. Osler's attorney, Ronald Peake, Esq., and Ms. Osler's granddaughter, Sarah Nicole Osler.

The matter is continued to Tuesday, June 16, 2009 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204 for proof of filing of the Petition for Court Approval of the Settlement Agreement. Appearances are waived if confirmation is filed before the June 16th hearing date.

Petition For Instructions Re Hand Written Instrument
Off Calendar May-07-2009 Continued To May-11-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 318
MINI MINUTES: Judge David L. Ballati, Clerk: Vicki Mack, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 (Department 318)

The cause comes on calendar regularly for Probate Court Trial on the issue re the validity of the June 20, 2005 7th Amendment to the Trust in the Matter of the 10/17/03 Revocable Trust of NATALIA S. CAPILI.
Appearances:
Charles Bruce Hamilton, Esq. is present, counsel for co-trustee Norman Arias (not present).
Michael Ellis Freedman, Esq. is present, counsel for co-trustee John Melone, also present.

Respective counsel represent to the Court that the matter has reached settlement. The Court accepts counsels' representation and directs counsel to provide a written agreement signed by the co-trustees, Norman Arias and John Melone, and Leila Osler no later than 9:00 AM on Monday, May 11, 2009. Fasimile signature pages attached to the agreement are acceptable.

The matter is continued to Monday, May 11, 2009 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 318 for the purpose of receiving the executed written agreement or going forth with the trial.

Petition For Order Approving Compensation Of Attorneys To Co-Trustee J. Melone.
Off Calendar May-05-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freeman for John Malone (Co-Trustee). Charles Hamilton for Norman Arias (Co-Trustee).

The Court stated that the hourly rate was reasonable and that the services appeared to be rendered. The Court granted both petitions, (signing the orders for the same in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition For Order Approving Compensation Of Attorney Of Co Trustee Norman Arias
Off Calendar May-05-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Ex Parte Application For Order Advancing Hearing Date And Shortening Time On Petition For Order Approving Compensation Of Attorney Of Co-Trustee Norman Arias
Off Calendar Apr-10-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Instructions Re Hand Written Instrument
Off Calendar Feb-03-2009 Continued To May-07-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 318
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for John Melone (Co-Trustee). Ronald Peake appeared by telephone for Lyla Osler (Beneficiary).

Mr. Freedman reported that mediation was unsuccessful and requested the matter be set for trial. The Court signed an order setting the matter for trial on the issue regarding validity of the June 20, 2005 hand written 7th amendment to the trust for May 7, 2009 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 204 and to continue on May 8, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept 204. The last day to conduct discovery is April 22, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.

Petition For Instructions Re Hand Written Instrument
Off Calendar Dec-02-2008 Continued To Feb-03-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for John Melone. Ronald Peake appeared by telephone for Leila Osler. Cesar Capili (Heir/Son) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Freedman stated that the petition requested the two Co-Trustees resolve the issue regarding the handwritten note, that there was a dispute between them and that neither one of them were beneficiaries but that their relatives were. In addition, he suggested setting a short cause trial for the Co-Trustees to provide validity/invalidity of the note, stated that there was a possibility of them resolving the issue and requested a status hearing be set in March. Mr. Peake requested the Court order the matter to mediation prior to March. All parties agreed to mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to pro bono mediation. Mediation is to be completed by January 28, 2009. Status on mediation is set for February 3, 2009. Mr. Capili requested the Court allow him to reside at the property located at 1822 42nd Street until the the property is sold stating that the Heirs agreed, that he would do repairs on the home and that he would vacate the premises upon request of the Court. Mr. Peake stated that Ms. Olser would object as there might be difficluty getting him out of the property once he moved in. The Court will leave this issue up to the Trustee to work out.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR 689. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
